title: Philippines protests Chinese coast guard water cannon attack injuring fishermen at Sabina Shoal

contents:
# Philippines protests Chinese coast guard water cannon attack injuring fishermen at Sabina Shoal

## Overview
Chinese coast guard ships blasted water cannons at Filipino fishermen and cut their anchor lines near Sabina Shoal. Three Filipino fishermen remained injured and two fishing vessels damaged in the incident. The Philippines announced it would formally protest the actions on December 15.

## Details
The event occurred in the Philippines' exclusive economic zone, 150km west of Palawan province, which China calls Xianbin Reef. Manila's coast guard reported the harassment over the weekend following the December 12 clash. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun claimed Philippine vessels rammed Chinese ships, ignored warnings, and involved threats with a knife. China's coast guard stated it drove away multiple Philippine vessels and took control measures. The Philippines described the water cannons and maneuvers as unjustifiable endangerment. The United States condemned China's use of water cannons and anchor line cutting.[1]

**Location**: Sabina Shoal, South China Sea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @NguyenThiHo88, which covers Vietnam South China Sea Chinese coast guard fishermen incidents. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Vietnam South China Sea Chinese coast guard fishermen incidents will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
